parameters.content:
COURTYARD-BHN | SALES-COLLECTOR
=====================================
Writes completed Courtyard NFT sales (Polygon) to eventhorizon.courtyard_sales.

SCHEDULE
--------
Every 30 min at :00 and :30.
Paired with COURTYARD-BHN | LISTINGS-COLLECTOR (runs at :15 / :45 → :00 / :30).
ARBITRAGE-SIGNALS workflow fires at :05 / :35 after both collectors.

FLOW
----
1. Schedule Trigger
2. HTTP Request → OpenSea events API (event_type=sale, limit=50)
   Auth: Header credential 'OpenSea API Key' → x-api-key header
3. Code: parse asset_events[] into one row per event, normalize fields
   (grader→codes, set_name→canonical 8, edition/print_variant→vocab,
    card_number strip '#' and '/N', sold_price = payment.quantity / 10^decimals)
4. Postgres Execute Query → INSERT ... resolve_card_id() inline,
   ON CONFLICT (item_id) DO NOTHING — natural dedup, no separate check needed.

DATA STANDARD
-------------
§10.2 — tokenized observation tables:
  edition + print_variant NOT NULL (defaults to N/A / Standard)
  grader codes only (PSA|CGC|BGS|SGC)
  grade TEXT verbatim raw_label (soft validate, no FK)
  listed_price = NULL on sales (this workflow)
  sold_price = USDC payment amount
  platform='courtyard', blockchain='polygon' — CHECK-pinned per table
  raw_payload = full event JSON for reprocessing if fields change

KNOWN GAPS
----------
- OpenSea API key required since 2026 — operator provisions credential.
- Single-page (limit=50) per run. Dedup catches dupes. If volume ever
  exceeds 50 sales / 30 min (currently it doesn't — Courtyard volume is
  ~$7.82M/week ≈ ~30 sales/hr peak), add pagination.
- resolve_card_id() may return NULL for unknown set_name drift — row
  still inserts with card_id=NULL, excluded from arbitrage only.

nodes.name: OpenSea Sales Events
nodes.notes:
OpenSea v2 events endpoint. Auth: 'OpenSea API Key' Header Auth credential — header name x-api-key, value = operator's OpenSea API key.

Free tier: ~4 req/sec, sufficient for /30-min schedule. Get key at https://docs.opensea.io/reference/api-keys

nodes.name: Parse & Normalize
nodes.notes:
Maps OpenSea asset_events → courtyard_sales rows.
Normalizes grader to codes, set_name to canonical 8, edition to vocab.
Skips non-graded NFTs (no grader attribute = not a Pokemon slab).
raw_payload preserves the original event for reprocessing if fields change.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO courtyard_sales (
  item_id, title, card_name, grader, grade, set_name, edition, print_variant,
  card_number, sold_price, listed_price, currency, transaction_type, sale_type,
  transaction_hash, seller_address, buyer_address, seller_username,
  platform, blockchain, nft_contract, item_creation_date, image_url, listing_url,
  raw_payload, card_id
) VALUES (
  $1, $2, NULLIF($3,'null'), $4, NULLIF($5,'null'), NULLIF($6,'null'), $7, $8,
  NULLIF($9,'null'), $10, NULL, 'USDC', 'SALE', 'peer_to_peer',
  $11, $12, $13, $14,
  'courtyard', 'polygon', '0x251be3a17af4892035c37ebf5890f4a4d889dcad',
  $15, $16, $17,
  $18::jsonb,
  resolve_card_id($3, $6, $9, $7, $8)
)
ON CONFLICT (item_id) DO NOTHING;

nodes.name: Insert courtyard_sales
nodes.notes:
Single round-trip per event: INSERT with resolve_card_id() inline + ON CONFLICT (item_id) DO NOTHING for natural dedup.

listed_price = NULL (sales events don't carry the pre-sale ask).
platform/blockchain/nft_contract hardcoded per §10.2 CHECK constraint.
sale_type defaults to 'peer_to_peer' (marketplace sales). Future: detect 'buyback' vs 'gacha' from event metadata.

If card_id resolves to NULL (set_name drift or no master_card_catalog match), the row still inserts — excluded from arbitrage signals only.
